articles.title: Trump cancels signing of bipartisan housing bill to pressure Congress to pass restrictive voting bill – US politics live
articles.description: President was due to sign bill aimed at making life more affordable for US voters ahead of midterms

articles.title: Europe heatwave live: UK breaks temperature record for June as parts of France hit 40C
articles.description: Surrey reports temperature of 35.7C, beating previous record from 1976; red weather alert extended to 72 of France’s 96 mainland departments

articles.title: France confirms first Ebola case in doctor who had worked in DRC
articles.description: French health ministry says patient’s contacts are being traced and that risk to European public is very low

more.title: SOWETAN | When medicinal plants disappear, SA loses more than biodiversity
more.description: South Africa’s conservation agenda has often treated medicinal plants with less urgency than more visible conservation priorities.

more.title: S’THEMBISO MSOMI | Ironic that patriotic Ibrahim will be buried in foreign land
more.description: On the eve of freedom in 1994, world-renowned music maestro Abdullah Ibrahim released his Knysna Blue album. It was, in part, a celebration of the country and what her people were about to achieve.

more.title: Thousands Processed in South African Border Repatriations Across Major Ports
more.description: South Africa — A major logistical operation involving South African border repatriations has facilitated the departure of thousands of foreign nationals across the country’s primary land and air ports. Border Management Authority (BMA) Commissioner Dr. Michael Masiapato confirmed the significant outward movement, which was executed over a five-day period through coordinated efforts with foreign missions [...]

more.title: Rwanda, South Africa: From diplomatic frost to strategic partnership
more.description: After more than a decade of diplomatic estrangement, Rwanda and South Africa appear to be taking meaningful steps toward rebuilding a relationship long marked by suspicion, political tensions, and missed opportunities. Recent high-level engagements between Kigali and Pretoria suggest a growing recognition that Africa’s future is better served by cooperation than confrontation. As both countries […]

more.title: Infrastructure backlog: Less than 17% of govt tenders actually awarded in 2025
more.description: Infrastructure SA has raised the alarm over the high level of tender cancellations. Stephen Grootes discusses the issue with Dean Macpherson, Minister of Public Works and Infrastructure.

more.title: What Bafana Bafana need to know about South Korea inn must-win World Cup Group A clash
more.description: Bafana Bafana face a critical match against South Korea, requiring a tactical masterclass to secure their Round of 32 qualification.

more.title: South Africans love to hate him, but here's Rassie's verdict on Henry Pollock
more.description: Springbok coach Rassie Erasmus says Henry Pollock's "awesome" output makes him a major threat as South Africa prepare to face England at Ellis Park.

more.title: Naspers Launches Free Agentic AI Platform ToqanClaw and Zapia Assistant for South African Businesses
more.description: Naspers, through its subsidiary Prosus, has launched a new agentic AI platform for businesses in South Africa called ToqanClaw — and the company says it is completely free to use, with no tokens or usage limits. Similar to the OpenClaw platform, ToqanClaw draws on multiple AI models from major providers, including Anthropic and other open-source [...]

more.title: Microsoft Brings MDASH Multi-Agent AI Security System to South African Customers as AI-Driven Cyber Threats Grow
more.description: As AI continues to accelerate cyber risk globally, Microsoft has announced a new multi-model, AI-driven vulnerability discovery system — codenamed MDASH and embedded with more than 100 individual agents — and is now onboarding South African customers into the programme. MDASH provides a harness for defenders to use multiple increasingly powerful models combined with agent [...]

more.title: Kenny Lattimore returns to Cape Town for one-night concert
more.description: Cape Town’s R&B fans are set for a nostalgic night of soulful ballads and timeless hits when Grammy-nominated singer Kenny Lattimore returns to the Mother City for a one-night-only performance later this year. The acclaimed American artist will take to the stage at the Grand Arena at GrandWest on 20 September, marking his first major Cape Town performance in more... more.title: PEPFAR Funding Withdrawal: South Africa Unsurprised by US Decision to Pull HIV Aid
more.description: NEW YORK — South Africa remains unsurprised by the PEPFAR funding withdrawal, with Health Minister Dr. Aaron Motsoaledi downplaying the US decision to pull critical HIV aid. Speaking on the sidelines of the UN High-Level Meeting on HIV and AIDS, the minister addressed the phased exit of the President’s Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief (PEPFAR) [...]

more.title: Korea coach undecided about how to set up against Bafana
more.description: South Korea’s coach has admitted he remains undecided on how he wants to set up his team for their crucial 2026 FIFA World Cup clash against Bafana Bafana.

more.title: Bafana star: We had to stay off social media...
more.description: Bafana Bafana winger Thapelo Maseko has revealed the severe extent of abuse suffered following the 2-0 defeat to Mexico in the opening match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

more.title: Jeffreys Bay’s surfing dreams dashed as World Surf League withdraws 2026 event
more.description: For decades, winter in Jeffreys Bay has meant more than chilly mornings and powerful swells. But in 2026, the familiar excitement is missing. Algoa FM News reports that the World Surf League (WSL) has removed Jeffreys Bay from its Championship Tour calendar, replacing the iconic South African stop with an event at New Zealand’s famed Raglan break. The decision, announced... Read more → The post Jeffreys Bay’s surfing dreams dashed as World Surf League withdraws 2026 event appeared first on Cape Town ETC .
